# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  def create
    uploaded_io = params[:image]
    format = params[:format] || 'jpg'
    size = params[:size] || '800x800'

    File.open(Rails.root.join('public', 'uploads', uploaded_io.original_filename), 'wb') do |file|
      file.write(uploaded_io.read)
    end

    input_path = Rails.root.join('public', 'uploads', uploaded_io.original_filename)
    output_path = Rails.root.join('public', 'uploads', "converted.#{format}")

    system("convert #{input_path} -resize #{size} #{output_path}")

    render json: { message: 'Image converted successfully', url: "/uploads/converted.#{format}" }, status: :ok
  rescue StandardError => e
    render json: { error: e.message }, status: :unprocessable_entity
  end
end
